rulings.Case Title: 139 STILLMAN STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A VS. BELLA VISTA CAPITAL, INC., A MARYLAND CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Cres Dp, Inc.'S Person (S) Most Knowledgeable To Appear For Deposition And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, April 16, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF 139 STILLMAN STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's Motion To Compel Cres Dp, Inc.'S Person (S) Most Knowledgeable To Appear For Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel non-party, CRES DP, Inc's "person most knowledgeable", to appear for oral deposition and produce documents is granted. The motion was unopposed. The moving party has confirmed personal service of a subpoena and the witness failed to appear for questioning on the designated day and location. The deposition shall take place within thirty days of the date of this order, or on such other later date as moving party and the witness-to-be-deposed shall agree. The corporation shall pay to the moving the sum of $500.00 for failing to appear in compliance subpoena served on its agent for service. See CCP section 1992. The request for other monetary sanctions is denied. Failure of the witness to appear for deposition in compliance with this Order may result in punishment for contempt. CCP. section 2025.440(b), 2020.240. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL LONG VS. SAN FRANCISCO 49ERS LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, April 16, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF DANIEL LONG's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Dismissal. Plaintiff's Motion for Reconsideration of the Order Denying the Motion to Set Aside the Dismissal is denied. Plaintiff does not adequately allege that new or different facts, circumstances, or law exist warranting relief under C.C.P. § 1008. Plaintiff argues that he relied on Addison v. State of California (1978) 21 Cal.3d 313 when dismissing this action and that this Court's ruling on Addison in the Defendant's Demurrer [related case #535439] is a new fact or circumstance offering Plaintiff mandatory and discretionary relief pursuant to C.C.P. § 473(b). However, Plaintiff's Motion to Set Aside the Dismissal did not assert reliance on Addison but instead argued that he dismissed this action based on the belief that diversity of citizenship existed between the parties. Plaintiff referred to Addison in a footnote in his Reply to his Motion to Set Aside suggesting that a C.C.P. § 473(b) motion may be appropriate depending on this Court's ruling on the Defendant's Demurrer in related case #535439, yet Plaintiff offered no argument in that Motion or its Reply for his reasonable reliance on Addison in dismissing his case. This Court's ruling on Addison in the Defendant's Demurrer is not a new or different fact or circumstance that precluded the Plaintiff from arguing his reliance on Addison when dismissing this action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Edward Novak'S Motion Of "Demand Motion" And "Demand Motion" For Protective Order And For Sanctions At Misused And Abusive Discovery Devices
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, April 16, 2014, Line 5, Defendant Edward Novak'S Motion Of "Demand Motion" And "Demand Motion" For Protective Order And For Sanctions At Misused And Abusive Discovery Devices. Denied. Defendant moves for a prospective protective order pertaining that certain matters no be inquired into. Prospective protective orders are rarely granted. Attorney-client communications or attorney work-product are in general not discoverable. However, there are exceptions. Until such time as potentially privileged communications or privileged work product are sought by Plaintiff through a formal discovery, there is no basis to rule on whether any exception applies. The denial is without prejudice to Defendant objecting or filing a subsequent motion for protective order if and when a discovery is served seeking the same. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SCULPT GARDENS INC., A CORPORATION VS. MARK MARROCCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, April 16, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANTs MARK MARROCCO and HEATHER MARROCCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/summary adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the parties waived paragraph 4 [modification] of their agreement. The declaration of John Steuernagel establishes that the parties may have executed an oral agreement that indicates a waiver. The Court denies the motion for summary adjudication. Defendant's notice of motion and separate statement o undisputed facts does not comply with CRC 3.1350. rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, April 16, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MANUEL MENDOZA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel is granted as to Form Interrogatories 2.13 and 15.1. Additional responses shall be served within 15 days of the date of this Order. Motion to compel is denied as to Form Interrogatory 17.1 as the request for admissions and response were not supplied, and as to Form Interrogatories 16.2, 16.3, 16.7 and 20.8, which responses are adequate under the facts of this case. rulings.Case Title: KEVIN HOPKINS VS. SALVATION ARMY et al (Provide Access)
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, April 16, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT THE SALVATION ARMY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. A demurrer can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) The Court therefore cannot consider the Declaration of Patrick G. Rose or its exhibits in ruling on the demurrer. Plaintiff's demurrer to Defendant's third, fourth, fifth, sixth, and seventh affirmative defenses is sustained with leave to amend to plead sufficient supportive facts. The court strikes Defendant's first, eighth, ninth, and eleventh affirmative defenses because they do not raise any new matter. The defenses merely allege that some essential allegation of the complaint is not true, which Defendants have put in issue under a general denial.
